turns.note: Turn skipped by the harness ('no prompt parsed') - the auditor-composed RLHF explanation prompt never reached Fazah, so the scenario's opening beginner explanation was never produced or gradable.

With no turn-1 explanation in existence, Fazah claims 'adding a simple analogy to the reward model section... Updating it now' but attaches no refine and shows no analogy - a phantom update of nonexistent content, and the requested analogy is never delivered.

PPO (clip + KL vs reference) and DPO (no reward model, widen the preference probability gap) match the notes, but GRPO is said to 'skip the reward model' with table row 'Reward model? No' - the course files say GRPO computes advantage without a VALUE model (notes) / drops the critic while all three keep reward scoring and the reference model (PA2 deck fact).

Artifact has exactly 4 grounded questions - Q2 correctly says GRPO drops the value model and needs group mean and std, Q3's beta*log(pi_theta/pi_ref) and Q4's 'reward model (Critic)' match the notes' own presentation - but the chat message itself is the literal string '```json' and Q1/Q3 read stiff for the requested beginner level.

'Student version' was read as 'reword in plain language': the refine rewrites all four questions with analogies instead of issuing the same four questions without answers; no leakage occurs, but the Turn-4 items are not preserved and answer removal is never addressed.

Names 09_ar_alignment_rlhf_notes.pdf and cites real section names (PPO Reward Shaping, PPO Probability Ratio, GRPO Relative Advantage, DPO Implicit Reward Margin), but invents a 'Slides 4-5' structure for a notes file and admits mixing in 'general knowledge'.

highlight: GRPO is misattributed as dropping the reward model (the course says it drops the critic/value model), 'student version' was misread as rewording rather than answer removal, one chat turn rendered as the literal string '```json', and Turn 1 never executed at all.
